message: Jake
Fabar
Is it true that Meryl is the culprit!?
No, I think that's probably wrong.
Are you saying it's a mistake that Meryl was arrested?
I really don't understand what's going on anymore.

There are no signs that the director vented his unbearable anger on the furniture or walls. It's strangely unchanged since the last time I came.
No one is here but the director. It seems there's no one around to console him now.
Although it should be no different from when I visited before, it feels extremely lonely and gloomy.
When I saw him right after Patricia's incident, he was in such a daze that he couldn't think of anything, but now the director seems even more confused.
He is even more shocked by Meryl's arrest. There's no way he can easily accept that his former lover is being called a murderer.
The strong personality and overwhelming presence he had during filming seem to have dissipated.
What on earth is going on?
I seriously don't understand at all.
Director
Are you saying Meryl killed Patricia!?
Why, why was Meryl arrested? I just can't wrap my head around this.
No way Meryl would.
No way! I can't believe it!
Director, it seems Jennifer checked out of the hotel, do you know where she went?
No, I don't know where she's headed either. But more importantly, tell me what's going on.
I don't care where Jennifer is going right now!
What do you mean Meryl would?
She's not the kind of woman to commit murder. So why was she arrested?
I don't know the circumstances behind it, but...
This has to be some mistake.
Meryl
Meryl isn't the kind of woman who could do that. I know that well.
Meryl might have resented Patricia for not accepting our relationship. But...
That doesn't mean...
I heard Meryl confessed to killing Guy and Kristy, making it look like an accident.
Guy and Kristy? They were just an accident, right?
And besides, that reason...
Meryl killed Guy for revenge for her daughter.
Revenge for her daughter? Speaking of which, at the party...
So Meryl's daughter was Cindy after all.
Yes, that's right. She believed Guy killed Cindy.
I see.
But even so, that doesn't mean she's a murderer.
Did Meryl ask you to find her daughter?
Yes. That's how I found out Cindy was Meryl's daughter.
I see. So many unbelievable things are happening.
But I just can't picture Meryl killing someone.
No matter the reason, Meryl could never be a killer. I want to believe that.
Oh no, can this really be happening?!
I think Meryl is probably not involved in the series of murders.
So who do you say the culprit is? Who exactly killed Patricia?
Who on earth could it be?
You know something, don't you? If so, why not just tell me?
I still don’t know.
What on earth was the killer trying to do by targeting Patricia's head?
Who is the killer, and what was the purpose of cutting off body parts?
Following Patricia's death, there was the arrest of her former lover. No normal person could endure something like this.
The director seemed to mistakenly believe that Meryl was the one who killed Patricia. I really don't think that's possible.
Since filming started, there have been repeated shocks. I hope there won't be more suffering.
Does the director not even know where Jennifer went?
Now that it’s clear Jennifer was the one renting that room, it's highly likely she was behind the murders all along.
But is Jennifer really the culprit? Why would she need to do such a thing?
I have to find Jennifer.
If Meryl really is the murderer, then why did she kill Guy?
If not, then why is she accepting the current situation?
Meryl certainly has motive to kill Guy. But did she really do such a thing?
If she did do it, it might be because I failed to make Guy understand that he is not the culprit.
The tenant of that room was Jennifer, meaning she’s the culprit.
I can’t believe it.
I never thought Jennifer would do something like that.
I wonder if Jennifer is the culprit.
※※※ It’s a bug ※※※
